messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
where is salt mined in the united states

content.message:
To determine where salt is mined in the United States, I need to identify entities related to "salt mining" and their locations. Let's proceed step-by-step.

1. First, I will search for entities related to "salt mining" or "salt" in the knowledge graph.
2. Then, I will check for properties related to "location" or "where" to retrieve places where salt is mined.
3. Finally, I will filter those results to focus on the United States.

### Step 1: Search for entities related to salt mining or salt
I'll search for "salt mining" in Wikidata to identify relevant entities or places tied to salt extraction.

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Salt mining (wd:Q12148489):
  - is subclass of mineral extraction
  - mining operation extracting rock salt or halite
2. Category:Salt mining (wd:Q9109836):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
3. Category:Salt mining museums (wd:Q130303465):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category
  - Wikimedia category
4. Finding Salt (wd:Q96575686), also known as "Looking for the Salt":
  - 1999 documentary film by Lungnan Isak Fangas
  - is instance of film
5. The Salt Lake Mining Review (wd:Q100281488, matched via "Salt Lake Mining Review"), also known as "Salt Lake Mining Review":
  - is instance of mining newspaper
  - newspaper published in Salt Lake City, Utah
6. garage sale (wd:Q19811, matched via "moving sale"), also known as "attic sale", "garbage sale", "junk sale", "lawn sale", "moving sale", etc.:
  - informal, irregularly scheduled event for the sale of used goods by private individuals
  - is subclass of market
7. Korean brining salt (wd:Q28454689), also known as "Korean sea salt":
  - coarse salt
  - is subclass of table salt
8. Viking Malt (wd:Q10715242):
  - is instance of business
9. Salo Mosing (wd:Q127990710):
  - is instance of river
  - river in Central Sulawesi, Indonesia
10. Salu Maiting (wd:Q127991951):
  - is instance of river
  - river in South Sulawesi, Indonesia

content.message:
The top result for "salt mining" is "Salt mining (wd:Q12148489)", which is a type of mineral extraction involving the extraction of rock salt or halite. This suggests that we should focus on this entity to explore where salt mining occurs, particularly in the United States.

### Step 2: Search for properties related to location or place of mining
I will now search for properties related to "location" or "place" in the context of salt mining to find where salt is mined.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. coordinate location (wd:P625 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "co-ordinate location", "co-ordinates", "coordinates", "co-ords", "coords", etc.:
  - geocoordinates of the subject. For Earth, please note that only the WGS84 geodetic datum is currently supported
2. location (wd:P276 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "based in", "city", "exact location", "from", "held by", etc.:
  - location of the object, structure or event; use P131 to ... entities; use P7153 for locations associated with the object
3. work location (wd:P937 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "active in", "conducts business at", "conducts business in", "did work at", "did work from", etc.:
  - location where persons or organisations were actively participating in employment, business or other work
4. valid in place (wd:P3005 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "applies to country", "applies to location", "applies to nonjurisdictional location", "applies to nonjurisdictional place", "applies to place", etc.:
  - place where a statement is valid
5. ranking (wd:P1352 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place"), also known as "peak position", "place", "placing", "position", "rank":
  - subject's ordinal position as qualitatively evaluated relative to other members of a group
6. occupant (wd:P466 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location of"), also known as "home team", "houses", "inhabitant", "lessee", "location of", etc.:
  - person or organization occupying property
7. applies to jurisdiction (wd:P1001 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of location"), also known as "applicable area", "applicable country", "applicable geographic place", "applicable jurisdiction", "applicable location", etc.:
  - the item (institution, law, public office, public register, ... jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
8. directions (wd:P2795 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "locality or place"), also known as "locality or place":
  - describe how to find the subject - directions, objects along way, comments
9. located in the present-day administrative territorial entity (wd:P3842 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "current location"), also known as "current administrative territorial entity", "current location", "currently in", "located in present day administrative territorial entity", "located in present-day administrative territorial entity", etc.:
  - the item was located in the territory of this present-day ... unit; however the two did not at any point coexist in time
10. located in the administrative territorial entity (wd:P131 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "locality"), also known as "administrative territorial entity", "administrative territory", "city", "happens in", "in", etc.:
  - the item is located on the territory of the following ... the item falls only partially into the administrative entity

content.message:
The most relevant property for identifying locations is "location" (wd:P276), which refers to the location of an entity or event. This property will help us find where salt is mined.

### Step 3: Search for properties of "salt mining" related to location
Now, I will search for properties related to "location" specifically in the context of "salt mining" (wd:Q12148489) to determine where salt is mined.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. has use (wd:P366 as wdt/p, matched via "as"), also known as "as", "can be used for", "for", "function", "had use", etc.:
  - main use of the subject (includes current and former usage)
2. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
3. subclass of (wd:P279 as wdt/p, matched via "type of"), also known as "all members of this class also belong to", "category of", "form of", "has superclass", "is a class of", etc.:
  - this item is a subclass (subset) of that item; ALL instances ... is a subclass of mountain; Everest is an instance of mountain
4. Google Knowledge Graph ID (wd:P2671 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "kgmid"), also known as "Google knowledge panel", "Google Knowledge Panel ID", "kgmid", "MREID":
  - identifier for Google Knowledge Graph API, starting with "/g/". For IDs starting with "/m/", use Freebase ID (P646)

content.message:
The search for properties of "salt mining" (wd:Q12148489) related to "location" did not yield any direct results. This suggests that "location" may not be a directly associated property for this entity. Instead, we need to explore the broader context of where salt is mined in the United States by searching for specific locations or places known for salt mining.

### Step 4: Search for entities related to salt mining in the United States
I will now search for entities related to salt mining specifically in the United States.
<tool_call>
{"name": "search_entity", "arguments": {"kg": "wikidata",://qlever.dev/api/wikidata", "query": "salt mining United States"}}
</tool_call>

question: where is salt mined in the united states
reference_answer: Hutchinson, Kansas | Avery Island, Louisiana | Detroit, Michigan | Saltville, Virginia | Western New York and Central New York
